preamble: The shift from the mid front unrounded vowel [e] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a process of glide formation and vocalization. It typically occurs through intermediate diphthongization (e.g., [ew] or [eu]) often triggered by neighboring labial or velarized consonants.

examples.note: Vocalization of the dark-L after a front vowel, creating a labiovelar glide.
Historical rounding and backing of the front vowel in the presence of [w] and [r].

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
